SMILE INDEX: 25
WINDOW: 468.367s to 507.233s (smile at 488.367s to 492.233s)

TRANSCRIPT CONTEXT:
[434.930-494.610] interviewee: for it. It's an order. I had to get dressed. I come to the gate. And the officer tells me, here is your pass. And somebody in front is waiting for you. I go to the gate and an older man. But I recognize right away he was not dressed like a European, like an American-- like clothes, fancy.
[494.610-518.200] interviewee: And he said, I'm your uncle. Now, this was in 1932. They came to visit us from the United States. And I took him out to the city. We went to eat. And we went to a Yiddish theatre

WORDS AT THE SMILE MOMENT:
like an American-- like clothes,
